data.title: US Banking Groups Request Delay on GENIUS Act Proposals
data.description: US banking groups have approached the Treasury Department and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. to postpone the comment deadlines for three GENIUS Act proposals. According to NS3.AI, the request was made on April 22, suggesting a delay until 60 days after the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency finalizes its framework. This move could potentially postpone the federal stablecoin rollout by several months. Additionally, banks are urging Senate lawmakers to impose stricter limits on stablecoin rewards as part of the CLARITY Act.

data.title: MegaETH will hold a TGE on April 30th.
data.description: MegaETH has announced the completion of its first Key Performance Indicator (KPI), with 10 "Mega Mafia" applications officially launched within its ecosystem and scheduled for TGE on April 30th. It is understood that MegaETH previously linked its token release mechanism to actual business performance, rather than the traditional time-based unlocking model. The first KPI required applications to have real user interaction capabilities, including a single application accumulating over 100,000 transactions within 30 days, conditions that have now been met. A MegaETH co-founder stated that this move aims to ensure the network has real-world use cases and economic cycles before token issuance, rather than simply relying on market expectations. Currently, the MegaETH ecosystem has incubated approximately 30 projects, including stablecoin payments, yield tokenization, and on-chain lending. The total supply of MEGA tokens is 10 billion, with 53.3% to be released gradually based on subsequent KPIs. (The Block)

data.title: Belarus plans to launch a crypto bank: supporting 26 types of crypto assets and 11 types of business operations.
data.description: Alexander Egorov, Deputy Governor of the National Bank of Belarus, stated that the country is advancing the construction of a "crypto bank" framework, planning to support approximately 26 crypto assets and 11 core business categories. According to relevant laws, crypto banks can use mainstream cryptocurrencies and stablecoins, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, Toncoin, and Solana. In terms of business scope, it covers 11 operations, including crypto deposits, crypto loans, staking, staking mining, asset transfers, self-issued tokens, trading and exchange, and custody. (Belta)

data.title: Belarus to Permit Operations of 'Crypto Banks' Supporting 26 Digital Assets
data.description: Belarus is set to allow the operation of 'crypto banks' starting April 24, according to BlockBeats. These institutions will support 26 digital assets, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, TON, and SOL. The services offered will encompass deposits, loans, staking, transfers, token issuance, and trading. This move marks a significant step in Belarus's approach to integrating cryptocurrency into its financial system.

data.title: Firelight and Sentora Introduce Vault for Enhanced On-Chain Security
data.description: Firelight and Sentora have announced the launch of Vault, a new on-chain security solution aimed at improving security standards for institutional funds operating on-chain. According to NS3.AI, the companies emphasize that this product is designed to bolster the security framework for institutional investors engaging in blockchain transactions.

data.title: U.S. Housing Market Shows Signs of Stabilization Amid Rate Stability
data.description: The U.S. housing market is beginning to show signs of stabilization as interest rates have steadied following a sharp increase earlier this year. Bloomberg posted on X that the rate surge was initially triggered by the onset of the Iran war in late February, which raised concerns about inflation. This stabilization in rates is providing a slight relief to the housing sector, which had been under pressure due to the earlier rate hikes.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 640 USDT with a Narrowed 1.43% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 23, 2026, 16:00 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 640 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 640.080017 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.43% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Israel Prepares for Potential Conflict with Iran, Awaits U.S. Approval
data.description: Israel's Defense Minister Katz announced on April 23 that the country is ready to resume military operations against Iran, pending approval from the United States. According to BlockBeats, Katz made this statement following a situation assessment, indicating that Israel is poised for action but requires a green light from the U.S. to proceed.

data.title: Bitcoin Approaches $80,000 Resistance Amid ETF Inflows and Whale Accumulation
data.description: Bitcoin is approaching the $80,000 mark, identified by market analysis as a significant resistance level for the ongoing rebound. According to NS3.AI, spot Bitcoin ETFs have experienced net inflows for six consecutive days. Additionally, whale addresses holding over 1,000 BTC have accumulated approximately 270,000 BTC in the past 30 days.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum options with a notional value of $9.87 billion will expire and settle on April 24.
data.description: Adam, a macro researcher at Greeks.live, published options settlement data on the X platform on April 24th: 1. 109,000 BTC options expired, with a put-call ratio of 0.93, a maximum price threshold of $72,000, and a notional value of $8.55 billion. 2. 563,000 ETH options expired, with a put-call ratio of 0.72, a maximum price threshold of $2,200, and a notional value of $1.32 billion. This week, the market continued its rebound, with Bitcoin strongly breaking through $78,000. The Web3 conference in Hong Kong was also filled with a bullish atmosphere, and altcoins are also recovering. This is monthly settlement, with 25% of options expiring. Regarding the holding period, the open interest distribution in the options market shows 12% at the end of May and 24% at the end of June. Looking at major options data, Bitcoin's primary implied volatility (IV) continued to decline this month, with most IVs falling by 1% to 2%, dropping below 40%. ETH's primary IV declined even more, currently hovering around 60%. Despite the price increase, skew has subsided, indicating a lack of FOMO (follow-on-demand) sentiment in the market. Bitcoin's performance in the second quarter of this year was significantly better than the first quarter in terms of both price and market activity. This month's continued rebound reflects capital inflows. If macroeconomic pressures bottom out by mid-year, then Bitcoin's bottom will be confirmed.

data.title: Morgan Stanley increased its holdings by 143.34 BTC, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 1964 BTC.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Morgan Stanley increased its holdings by 143.34 BTC about an hour ago, worth $11.17 million, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 1,964.

data.title: Israel says it is ready to restart war with Iran
data.description: Israeli Defense Minister Katz stated at the conclusion of a situation assessment on the 23rd that Israel is prepared to resume war with Iran, "waiting only for the green light from the United States." (CCTV News)

data.title: Indonesian Rupiah and Indian Rupee Experience Decline Against US Dollar
data.description: The Indonesian rupiah has depreciated to 17,315 against the US dollar, while the Indian rupee has weakened to 94.08. According to NS3.AI, the Reserve Bank of India previously set a limit on bank bets against the rupee at $100 million and urged banks to liquidate US dollar holdings before April 10. Meanwhile, the Bank of Indonesia announced its commitment to implementing all necessary measures to stabilize the rupiah.

data.title: Pakistani diplomat: US-Iran negotiations "at a standstill" with "very slow" progress.
data.description: Saudi Arabia's Al Arabiya television, citing a Pakistani diplomat, reported that negotiations between the United States and Iran have "reached a stalemate" and are progressing "very slowly." The diplomat said the United States remains committed to maintaining its naval blockade of Iran. Iran has informed Pakistan that the US blockade is an obstacle to its participation in the negotiations. (Xinhua)

data.title: AI TRENDS | Weyerhaeuser Embraces AI for Transformative Forestry Practices
data.description: Weyerhaeuser, the leading logging company in the United States, is investing in artificial intelligence to revolutionize forestry operations. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the company aims to leverage AI technology to enhance efficiency and sustainability in its logging practices. By integrating AI, Weyerhaeuser seeks to optimize resource management and improve environmental outcomes, marking a significant shift in the industry. This move reflects a broader trend of incorporating advanced technologies to address ecological challenges and streamline operations. As AI continues to evolve, its application in forestry could set new standards for sustainable practices and operational excellence.

data.title: Analysis: BTC is approaching the key $80,000 level, supported by institutional funds and whale buying, but whether it can break through further remains to be seen.
data.description: Bitcoin is approaching the $80,000 mark again, and market analysts believe this level has become a key resistance level to test the strength of this rebound. On the funding front, continued institutional inflows are providing support. Data shows that Bitcoin spot ETFs have seen net inflows for six consecutive days, and Ethereum spot ETFs have also recorded nine consecutive days of increases, indicating a recovery in risk appetite. Meanwhile, whale addresses holding over 1,000 BTC have accumulated approximately 270,000 BTC in the past 30 days, marking the largest monthly increase since 2013, and exchange reserves have fallen to a seven-year low. On the on-chain data front, Glassnode points out that Bitcoin has regained its "real market average price" (approximately $78,100), but short-term holders' costs are around $80,100, forming a direct resistance zone. Once the price reaches this range, over 54% of short-term investors will be in a profitable position, historically often corresponding to a peak in the rebound phase. Meanwhile, perpetual contract funding rates remain negative, indicating heavy short positions. With continued improvement in spot demand, this could provide upward pressure for short covering. Overall, although funding structures and market resilience have improved, $80,000 remains a key level, and the market has yet to confirm whether it can transform from resistance to support. (The Block)

data.title: ILITY raises $2 million in seed funding, led by Animoca Brands.
data.description: ILITY announced the completion of a $2 million seed round of financing at a valuation of $21 million. The round was led by Animoca Brands, with participation from DAO Duck and others. The funds will primarily be used to accelerate protocol development and expand ecosystem partnerships. The project aims to build a first-layer blockchain protocol focused on private cross-chain verification, achieving trustless verification of holdings and actions through zero-knowledge proofs while maintaining wallet address privacy.

data.title: MagicBlock Introduces Privacy Payment Tool for Solana Network
data.description:
MagicBlock has launched Mirage, a command-line privacy payment tool for the Solana network. According to Foresight News, this tool allows users to create wallets, deposit funds, and send private transactions via terminal, bots, or AI agents.

Mirage is built on Private Ephemeral Rollups and currently supports private SPL token transfers. It offers features such as up to 15 transaction splits to obscure amounts and random delays to conceal timing information. Additionally, it can be installed as a Claude Code Skill. Future plans include the development of stealth address functionalities.

data.title: Market Shifts as Kelp Incident Spurs 'Refugee Trade'
data.description:
The recent Kelp incident has led to significant market shifts, according to ChainCatcher. On-chain analytics platform Santiment reported a noticeable trend of 'refugee trade' six days after the event.

The Spark token (SPK) experienced a dramatic increase, doubling in value from $0.029 to $0.058 within 48 hours. Concurrently, large transactions exceeding $100,000 surged from an average of about 30 per day to 183 today.

In contrast, the AAVE token has seen a continuous decline, currently hovering around $92, despite Bitcoin surpassing $79,000 earlier this week.

Santiment highlighted that the incident did not merely cause disruption but also resulted in a redistribution of funds. A liquidity crisis in one protocol has turned into a bull market for another, illustrating the dynamics of 'refugee trade' in the crypto and DeFi sectors. This term metaphorically describes the movement of funds from damaged or high-risk protocols to relatively safer ones.

data.title: Aave has suspended rsETH reserve operations on Ethereum, Arbitrum, and other networks to reduce systemic risk.
data.description: Aave has released an update on the rsETH security incident on its X platform, disclosing that it has suspended rsETH reserve operations on the Ethereum mainnet and networks such as Arbitrum, Base, Mantle, and Linea. This measure aims to preserve as much capital as possible and reduce systemic risk during the asset recovery process. Aave stated that it will continue to disclose further developments and solutions to the community.
